frameworks.name: deep learning
frameworks.one_line: End-to-end neural network pipeline applied to raw video frames for behavior classification.

frameworks.name: computer vision pose estimation
frameworks.one_line: Segmentation, identification and pose estimation modules combined into one pipeline.

core_question: Can a single deep-learning pipeline segment, identify, pose-estimate, and classify individual and social animal behavior in complex home-cage environments from raw monocular video?
key_assumptions: Raw video frames contain sufficient signal for behavior classification without hand-crafted features
Initial human supervision suffices; no further intervention needed at inference
Mono-vision cameras in home cages capture enough of 3D social interaction
manipulations: Species (individual mice vs socially interacting non-human primates)
Setting (single-animal vs multi-animal social complex environment)
key_findings: SIPEC pipeline performs segmentation, identification, pose estimation, and behavior classification end-to-end
Outperforms prior state of the art on these tasks
Recognizes multiple behaviors of freely moving mice and of socially interacting primates in 3D using only mono-vision home-cage video
interpretations: Group and social animal behavior in complex environments is tractable with a single deep architecture trained on raw frames
Minimal human supervision is enough for the pipeline to generalize across species and settings
